text:
{
  "english": {
    "IsA": [
      "It is an Eastern Orthodox icon of the Virgin Mary.",
      "It is a prototype which has been copied numerous times."
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "It has a unique characteristic of what appears to be a scar on the Virgin Mary's right cheek or her chin.",
      "It has a chased riza of silver and gold covering almost all the figures except the faces."
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "It is characterized by being \"Wonderworking\".",
      "It is characterized by a scar on the Virgin Mary's right cheek or her chin.",
      "It is characterized by a chased riza of silver and gold covering almost all the figures except the faces."
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "It is used for veneration.",
      "It is used for commemorating the appearance of the miraculous myrrh.",
      "It is used for commemorating the translation to Moscow.",
      "It is used for commemorating the translation to Valday Iversky Monastery.",
      "It is used for commemorating the appearance of the Iverion Icon of the Mother of God.",
      "It is used for commemorating the return of the icon from the museum to Dormition Cathedral, Moscow.",
      "It is used for commemorating the translation to Iberian Gate."
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "It is located in the Georgian Iveron monastery on Mount Athos in Greece.",
      "It is located in a small Chapel next to the Gates.",
      "It is located next to the Kremlin walls in Moscow.",
      "It is located in the Dormition Cathedral, Moscow."
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "It was built in 1669."
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "It originated in Constantinople.",
      "It originated in Nicaea."
    ],
    "FoundedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "FoundedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "CreatedBy": [
      "It was created by Luke the Evangelist."
    ],
    "CreatedIn": [

    "DevelopedBy": [

    ],

    ],
    "DiscoveredBy": [
      "It was discovered by Gabriel the Iberian."
    ],
    "DiscoveredIn": [
      "It was discovered in the Mediterranean Sea.",
      "It was discovered in 1004."
    ],
    "InventedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "InventedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "PublishedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],

    ],
    "ProducedBy": [
      "It was produced by Patriarch Nikon of Moscow."
    ],
    "CausedBy": [
      "It is caused by the intercession of the Theotokos.",
      "It is caused by the stabbing by a soldier in Nicaea.",
      "It is caused by the miracle of the bleeding wound.",
      "It is caused by the appearance of the miraculous myrrh."
    ],
    "Affects": [
      "It affects persons praying before it.",
      "It affects the faithful with numerous miracles.",
      "It affects the Tsar down to the lowest peasant."
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "It consists of figures of the Virgin Mary and Christ Child."
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "It is part of the Hodegetria family of images of the Theotokos.",
      "It is part of the Iveron monastery.",
      "It is part of the katholikon of the monastery.",
      "It is part of the Gates of the monastery.",
      "It is part of the Iverskaya Chapel.",
      "It is part of the Dormition Cathedral, Moscow."
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "It is a member of the Hodegetria family of images."
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "It belongs to the Eastern Orthodox Church.",
      "It belongs to the Iveron monastery."
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "It is known for numerous miracles attributed to it.",
      "It is known for a unique scar on the Virgin Mary's cheek or chin.",
      "It is known for being \"Wonderworking\".",
      "It is known for the tradition of being cast into the sea.",
      "It is known for the tradition of appearing on the monastery gates.",
      "It is known for the tradition of being called \"Gate-Keeper\".",
      "It is known for the Moscow version which was commissioned by Patriarch Nikon.",
      "It is known for the myrrh-streaming icon from Montreal.",
      "It is known for the miracle of blood flowing from a wound.",
      "It is known for the miracle of myrrh streaming."
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "It is associated with Luke the Evangelist.",
      "It is associated with the Iveron monastery.",
      "It is associated with Mount Athos.",
      "It is associated with Gabriel the Iberian.",
      "It is associated with the Iveron icon.",
      "It is associated with the Iverskaya Chapel.",
      "It is associated with Patriarch Nikon of Moscow.",
      "It is associated with Brother José Muñoz Cortés.",
      "It is associated with the Akathist to the Mother of God.",
      "It is associated with the Byzantine iconoclasm."
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "It is named after the Gates of the monastery.",
      "It is named after the Iveron monastery."
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "It occurred in Nicaea.",
      "It occurred in the Mediterranean Sea.",
      "It occurred on Mount Athos.",
      "It occurred in Moscow.",
      "It occurred in Montreal.",
      "It occurred in Athens, Greece."
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "It was held in the Iveron monastery.",
      "It was held in the katholikon of the monastery.",
      "It was held in a small Chapel next to the Gates.",
      "It was held in the Iverskaya Chapel.",
      "It was held in the Dormition Cathedral, Moscow.",
      "It was held in the museum.",
      "It was held in a hotel room in Athens, Greece."
    ],
    "WonBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "About": [
      "It is about the Virgin Mary."
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "It was influenced by the prototype from Constantinople.",
      "It was influenced by the Hodegetria family of images.",
      "It was influenced by the Emperor Theophilus.",
      "It was influenced by the Akathist to the Mother of God."
    ],
    "Influenced": [

      "It influenced the Our Lady of Częstochowa icon.",
      "It influenced the Iverskaya Chapel.",
      "It influenced the veneration practices in the Orthodox Church."

  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それは聖母マリアの東方正教会のイコンです。",
      "それは何度も複製されてきたプロトタイプです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それは聖母マリアの右頬または顎にある傷のように見える独特の特徴を持っています。",
      "それは顔以外のほとんどすべての人物を覆う、銀と金の打ち出し細工のリザを特徴としています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それは「奇跡を起こす」という特徴があります。",
      "それは聖母マリアの右頬または顎の傷という特徴があります。",
      "それは顔以外のほとんどすべての人物を覆う、銀と金の打ち出し細工のリザという特徴があります。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは崇敬のために使用されます。",
      "それは奇跡的な聖油の出現を記念するために使用されます。",
      "それはモスクワへの遷座を記念するために使用されます。",
      "それはヴァルダイ・イヴェルスキー修道院への遷座を記念するために使用されます。",
      "それは聖母のイヴェルリャンカのイコンの出現を記念するために使用されます。",
      "それはモスクワの生神女就寝大聖堂へのイコンの返還を記念するために使用されます。",
      "それはイベリア門への遷座を記念するために使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それはギリシャのアトス山のイヴェル修道院にあります。",
      "それは門の隣の小さな礼拝堂にあります。",
      "それはモスクワのクレムリンの壁の隣にあります。",
      "それはモスクワの生神女就寝大聖堂にあります。"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "それは1669年に建てられました。"
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "それはコンスタンティノープルで生まれました。",
      "それはニカイアで生まれました。"
    ],
    "FoundedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "FoundedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "CreatedBy": [
      "それは福音記者ルカによって作成されました。"
    ],
    "CreatedIn": [
      "それは999年に作成されました。"
    ],
    "DevelopedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DevelopedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DiscoveredBy": [
      "それはガブリエル・イベリア人によって発見されました。"
    ],
    "DiscoveredIn": [
      "それは地中海で発見されました。",
      "それは1004年に発見されました。"
    ],
    "InventedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "InventedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "PublishedIn": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "WrittenBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ComposedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "DirectedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "PerformedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ManufacturedBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "ProducedBy": [
      "それはモスクワのニコン総主教によって制作されました。"
    ],
    "CausedBy": [
      "それは神の母の執り成しによって引き起こされます。",
      "それはニカイアの兵士による刺し傷によって引き起こされます。",
      "それは血を流す傷の奇跡によって引き起こされます。",
      "それは奇跡的な聖油の出現によって引き起こされます。"
    ],
    "Affects": [
      "それはそれの前で祈る人々に影響を与えます。",
      "それは信者に多くの奇跡をもたらします。",
      "それは皇帝から最も低い農民まで影響を与えます。"
    ],
    "ConsistsOf": [
      "それは聖母マリアと幼子キリストの姿で構成されています。"
    ],
    "PartOf": [
      "それは神の母のホデゲトリアのイコンの一族の一部です。",
      "それはイヴェル修道院の一部です。",
      "それは修道院の本堂の一部です。",
      "それは修道院の門の一部です。",
      "それはイヴェルスカヤ礼拝堂の一部です。",
      "それはモスクワの生神女就寝大聖堂の一部です。"
    ],
    "MemberOf": [
      "それはホデゲトリアのイコンの一族の一員です。"
    ],
    "BelongsTo": [
      "それは東方正教会に属しています。",
      "それはイヴェル修道院に属しています。"
    ],
    "KnownFor": [
      "それはそれに帰せられる多くの奇跡で知られています。",
      "それは聖母マリアの頬または顎の独特の傷で知られています。",
      "それは「奇跡を起こす」ことで知られています。",
      "それは海に投げ込まれたという伝承で知られています。",
      "それは修道院の門に現れたという伝承で知られています。",
      "それは「門番」と呼ばれるという伝承で知られています。",
      "それはニコン総主教によって命じられたモスクワ版で知られています。",
      "それはモントリオールの聖油を流すイコンで知られています。",
      "それは傷から血が流れる奇跡で知られています。",
      "それは聖油が流れる奇跡で知られています。"
    ],
    "AssociatedWith": [
      "それは福音記者ルカと関連があります。",
      "それはイヴェル修道院と関連があります。",
      "それはアトス山と関連があります。",
      "それはガブリエル・イベリア人と関連があります。",
      "それはイヴェルリャンカのイコンと関連があります。",
      "それはイヴェルスカヤ礼拝堂と関連があります。",
      "それはモスクワのニコン総主教と関連があります。",
      "それはホセ・ムニョス・コルテス兄弟と関連があります。",
      "それは聖母へのアカイスト賛歌と関連があります。",
      "それはビザンティン聖像破壊運動と関連があります。"
    ],
    "NamedAfter": [
      "それは修道院の門にちなんで名付けられました。",
      "それはイヴェル修道院にちなんで名付けられました。"
    ],
    "OccurredIn": [
      "それはニカイアで起こりました。",
      "それは地中海で起こりました。",
      "それはアトス山で起こりました。",
      "それはモスクワで起こりました。",
      "それはモントリオールで起こりました。",
      "それはギリシャのアテネで起こりました。"
    ],
    "HeldIn": [
      "それはイヴェル修道院で保持されていました。",
      "それは修道院の本堂で保持されていました。",
      "それは門の隣の小さな礼拝堂で保持されていました。",
      "それはイヴェルスカヤ礼拝堂で保持されていました。",
      "それはモスクワの生神女就寝大聖堂で保持されていました。",
      "それは博物館で保持されていました。",
      "それはギリシャのアテネのホテルの部屋で保持されていました。"
    ],
    "WonBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "About": [
      "それは聖母マリアについてです。"
    ],
    "InfluencedBy": [
      "それはコンスタンティノープルの原型に影響を受けました。",
      "それはホデゲトリアのイコンの一族に影響を受けました。",
      "それはテオフィロス皇帝に影響を受けました。",
      "それは聖母へのアカイスト賛歌に影響を受けました。"
    ],
    "Influenced": [
      "それはホデゲトリアのイコンの一族に影響を与えました。",
      "それはチェンストホバの聖母のイコンに影響を与えました。",
      "それはイヴェルスカヤ礼拝堂に影響を与えました。",
      "それは正教会の崇敬の慣習に影響を与えました。"
    ]
  }
}

IsA: It is an Eastern Orthodox icon of the Virgin Mary.
It is a prototype which has been copied numerous times.
HasA: It has a unique characteristic of what appears to be a scar on the Virgin Mary's right cheek or her chin.
It has a chased riza of silver and gold covering almost all the figures except the faces.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a scar on the Virgin Mary's right cheek or her chin.
It is characterized by a chased riza of silver and gold covering almost all the figures except the faces.
UsedFor: It is used for commemorating the appearance of the miraculous myrrh.
It is used for commemorating the translation to Moscow.
It is used for commemorating the translation to Valday Iversky Monastery.
It is used for commemorating the appearance of the Iverion Icon of the Mother of God.
It is used for commemorating the return of the icon from the museum to Dormition Cathedral, Moscow.
It is used for commemorating the translation to Iberian Gate.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Georgian Iveron monastery on Mount Athos in Greece.
It is located in a small Chapel next to the Gates.
It is located next to the Kremlin walls in Moscow.
It is located in the Dormition Cathedral, Moscow.
CreatedBy: It was created by Luke the Evangelist.
CreatedIn: It was created in the year 999.
DiscoveredBy: It was discovered by Gabriel the Iberian.
DiscoveredIn: It was discovered in the Mediterranean Sea.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Patriarch Nikon of Moscow.
CausedBy: It is caused by the intercession of the Theotokos.
It is caused by the stabbing by a soldier in Nicaea.
It is caused by the miracle of the bleeding wound.
It is caused by the appearance of the miraculous myrrh.
Affects: It affects persons praying before it.
It affects the faithful with numerous miracles.
It affects the Tsar down to the lowest peasant.
ConsistsOf: It consists of figures of the Virgin Mary and Christ Child.
PartOf: It is part of the Hodegetria family of images of the Theotokos.
It is part of the Iveron monastery.
It is part of the katholikon of the monastery.
It is part of the Gates of the monastery.
It is part of the Iverskaya Chapel.
It is part of the Dormition Cathedral, Moscow.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Hodegetria family of images.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Eastern Orthodox Church.
It belongs to the Iveron monastery.
KnownFor: It is known for numerous miracles attributed to it.
It is known for a unique scar on the Virgin Mary's cheek or chin.
It is known for being "Wonderworking".
It is known for the tradition of being cast into the sea.
It is known for the tradition of appearing on the monastery gates.
It is known for the tradition of being called "Gate-Keeper".
It is known for the Moscow version which was commissioned by Patriarch Nikon.
It is known for the myrrh-streaming icon from Montreal.
It is known for the miracle of blood flowing from a wound.
It is known for the miracle of myrrh streaming.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Luke the Evangelist.
It is associated with the Iveron monastery.
It is associated with Mount Athos.
It is associated with Gabriel the Iberian.
It is associated with the Iveron icon.
It is associated with the Iverskaya Chapel.
It is associated with Patriarch Nikon of Moscow.
It is associated with Brother José Muñoz Cortés.
It is associated with the Akathist to the Mother of God.
It is associated with the Byzantine iconoclasm.
NamedAfter: It is named after the Gates of the monastery.
It is named after the Iveron monastery.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the Mediterranean Sea.
HeldIn: It was held in the Iveron monastery.
It was held in the katholikon of the monastery.
It was held in a small Chapel next to the Gates.
It was held in the Iverskaya Chapel.
It was held in the Dormition Cathedral, Moscow.
It was held in the museum.
It was held in a hotel room in Athens, Greece.
About: It is about the Virgin Mary.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the prototype from Constantinople.
It was influenced by the Hodegetria family of images.
It was influenced by the Emperor Theophilus.
It was influenced by the Akathist to the Mother of God.
Influenced: It influenced the Hodegetria family of images.
It influenced the Our Lady of Częstochowa icon.
It influenced the veneration practices in the Orthodox Church.
